reasoning: Line 349 of class-plugin-usage-tracker.php includes 'file_location' => __FILE__ in the $body array returned by get_data(). This array is passed unmodified to send_data(), which forwards it to remote_post(), which POSTs it as the HTTP body to https://send.wpinsight.com/process-plugin-data via wp_remote_post(). No field stripping, deny-listing, or sanitization removes 'file_location' anywhere in the chain. The diff() method (line 588) only skips unchanged fields on subsequent calls; on the initial registration call (site_id == false, line 471), the full $body including file_location is always transmitted. __FILE__ expands to the absolute server filesystem path of the tracker class (e.g. /var/www/html/wp-content/plugins/disable-comments/includes/class-plugin-usage-tracker.php), leaking the document root and directory layout to the third-party telemetry operator. Reachability is automatic (WP-Cron and deactivation hook), not attacker-triggered, and is gated by is_tracking_allowed() which requires opt-in consent. The disclosure is to the telemetry service, not a direct network attacker. Severity is low: no RCE or auth bypass results, but the path exposure is unnecessary and real.
